role: A hand-rolled global subscription store and React hook that fetch and hold the session list and per-session recall lens data for the session-drill surface.
capabilities: Fetch session list from /api/sessions with fail-loud error handling
Maintain global UI state: open/closed, selected session, loading, error
Fetch per-session lens from /api/session-recall concurrently for catch_up, open_loops, and directives
Apply a 4-second per-lens fetch timeout and degrade empty on failure
Expose useSessions hook that subscribes components to the global state and auto-starts list load
substance_why: The file implements real fetch logic, typed domain shapes, concurrent lens loading, error states, and a complete subscription lifecycle, not just stubs.
interactions_present: useSessions mount auto-loads the session list
openSessions opens the surface and reloads the list
selectSession drills into a session and triggers lens fetch
interactions_missing: No explicit retry action after list load failure
No refresh/reload control for the list
No pagination, sorting, or filtering UI wired
Only three lens ops fetched; decisions/drift/timeline/find ops are not loaded
No search/find interaction for the lens
No per-lens loading or error visibility; failures silently become empty arrays
No cancellation/deduplication if the same session is selected repeatedly
performance_risks: Global mutable state with a Set-of-callbacks subscription pattern causes every subscriber to re-render on every state change
No request deduplication for loadLens, so rapid re-selections can spawn overlapping fetches
Three concurrent lens fetches per selection with a fixed 4-second timeout can create request bursts
No caching of sessions or lens data, so reopening or reselecting repeats network work
Large session/lens JSON is parsed and mapped on the main thread with no virtualization
reuse_why: The grounded SessionRow/SessionLens types and the /api/sessions and /api/session-recall fetch contracts should be preserved, but the hand-rolled mutable subscription store should be rebuilt with a modern state solution.
decomposition_hints: Split list state from lens state so the list does not re-render when a lens updates
Extract API adapters for /api/sessions and /api/session-recall into separate client modules
Separate the imperative store from the useSessions hook/presentation binding
Decompose lens loading per op (catch_up, open_loops, directives, decisions, drift, timeline, find) instead of one hard-coded batch
Move pure UI state (open/selected) out of the data-fetching store
